forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; Metaculus ~25% by 2029; Kalshi 40%; Samotsvety 28%; no material shift
forecasts.reference_class: Historical forecasting platform consensus on transformative AI milestones
forecasts.base_rate: Metaculus 25% by 2029; Kalshi 40% by 2030; Samotsvety ~28% by 2030

factors_up.factor: AI lab CEO statements pointing to AGI within 2-5 years
Rapid capability gains in coding/math; Shane Legg 50% by 2028
Kalshi market pricing 40% by 2030

factors_down.factor: Metaculus stricter definition includes robotics (lagging); depresses estimate
Key technical barriers: persistent memory, world models, novel reasoning
Contested definition; labs may claim AGI prematurely

key_uncertainties: Definition of AGI varies dramatically across resolution criteria
Whether current scaling trajectory continues or hits wall

update_triggers.up: Major lab publicly claims AGI with broad expert acceptance; breakthrough in autonomous R&D
update_triggers.down: Scaling laws empirically plateau; key benchmark progress stalls for 6+ months

forecasts.question: Will economic consultancy lose ≥33% value/jobs by end 2030 due to AI?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; AI productivity paradox documented but billable-hours model slows aggregate decline; 33% unlikely
forecasts.reference_class: Industry revenue/employment decline from prior technology disruptions
forecasts.base_rate: Graduate hiring down 6-30%; McKinsey using AI for 30% time savings but revenue model intact

factors_up.factor: Entry-level hiring cut 6-30% at Big Four; McKinsey planning 10% workforce cuts
65% of enterprises saying traditional consulting losing value
AI agents could displace entire project teams by 2028-2030

factors_down.factor: Productivity paradox: firms resist full AI deployment to protect billable hours
33% aggregate revenue decline requires structural model collapse, not efficiency gains
Demand for high-skill advisory roles rising; firms pivoting to outcome-based pricing

key_uncertainties: Speed at which AI agents can replace senior-level reasoning and client relationships
Whether clients demand AI cost savings or firms absorb productivity gains as margin

update_triggers.up: Major consultancy announces >15% revenue decline attributed to AI; sector-wide employment data shows 20%+ drop
update_triggers.down: Consultancies successfully transition to AI-augmented model with stable/growing revenues

forecasts.question: Will Labour be largest party at next UK General Election?
forecasts.key_change: ↓ Reform holds 8pt lead (30% vs 22% Labour, Ipsos Jan); MRP projects Reform 335-seat majority; May 2026 elections loom
forecasts.reference_class: UK opposition parties recovering from large polling deficits within 3-year election window
forecasts.base_rate: Historically rare for trailing party to recover from 8+ point deficit to win most seats

factors_up.factor: 3+ years remain before election deadline (Aug 2029); large swings possible
Only 25% agree Reform ready to govern; competence doubts may crystallise
Tactical voting could cost Reform dozens of seats even on current numbers
Labour at 22% with partial recovery underway from 18% trough

factors_down.factor: Reform at 30% with structural lead sustained 10+ months; not a temporary spike
MRP projects Reform 335-seat majority; Labour potentially third place
Labour PM Starmer at -62 net approval; leadership crisis risk from May 2026 locals
Multi-party fragmentation (Greens 12-17%) splits progressive vote

key_uncertainties: Whether May 2026 local elections trigger Labour leadership change
Whether Reform's government-readiness gap widens or closes

update_triggers.up: Reform drops below 25% in sustained polling; Labour leadership change galvanises support
update_triggers.down: Labour finishes third in May 2026 locals; leadership challenge materialises and fails

forecasts.question: Will AGI be developed before 1 January 2035?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; Metaculus median AGI 2033 implies ~65% by 2035; combined forecast dashboard median 2031
forecasts.reference_class: Forecasting platform consensus on AGI arrival dates
forecasts.base_rate: Metaculus 50% by 2033; combined forecast dashboard median 2031; both imply 65-70% by 2035

factors_up.factor: Combined forecast dashboard median 2031 — well inside 2035 window
AI lab CEOs shortening timelines to 2-5 years from 2025 baseline
Manifold 47% probability of AGI before 2028 (deep inside 2035 window)

factors_down.factor: Technical barriers may stall progress: world models, causal reasoning, embodied intelligence
Definition uncertainty: stricter definitions push timelines further out
Historical expert forecasts have been systematically early

key_uncertainties: Whether compute scaling continues or faces diminishing returns
Whether multi-modal/agentic systems cross key capability thresholds before 2035

update_triggers.up: AGI announced before 2030 (auto-resolves YES); major breakthrough in autonomous reasoning
update_triggers.down: Fundamental capability stall across all frontier labs sustained for 2+ years

forecasts.question: Will China initiate a military blockade or invasion of Taiwan before 1 January 2030?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; PLA purges limit capability for 2+ years; US intel reaffirms non-military preference; no escalation signals
forecasts.reference_class: Historical base rate of major-power military initiations against peer/near-peer rivals
forecasts.base_rate: US intel community: China prefers unification without force; amphibious assault seen as high-risk/high-failure

factors_up.factor: PLA has prepositioned missiles, air defenses for rapid blockade initiation
Gray zone escalation (ADIZ violations, cyber) on upward trend
Xi's stated unification goal and potential third-term political pressure

factors_down.factor: PLA purges at highest levels suppress capability for at least 2 years (Eurasia Group)
US intel March 2026 reaffirms China not committed to invasion timeline
Cost calculus: would derail China's economic development, leadership's top priority
Amphibious assault across 90-mile strait still logistically extremely difficult

key_uncertainties: Speed of PLA capability recovery post-purges
Whether Taiwan independence declaration or US policy shift creates crisis trigger

update_triggers.up: PLA conducts full-scale military exercise encircling Taiwan; Xi makes explicit deadline statement
update_triggers.down: US-China military hotline upgraded; formal cross-strait dialogue resumes

forecasts.question: Will UK annual CPI inflation fall below 2.0% for at least one month before 1 January 2027?
forecasts.key_change: ↓↓ Feb 2026 CPI at 3.0%; BoE Q2 forecast only 2.1%; Capital Economics sub-2% in April is outlier; revising sharply from 72%
forecasts.reference_class: UK inflation returning to target following supply-side shocks
forecasts.base_rate: OBR: 2.1% in Q4 2026; BoE: 2.1% in Q2 2026; Capital Economics outlier: sub-2% in April

factors_up.factor: Ofgem cap cut ~16% in April 2026; energy ~4-5% of CPI basket; ~0.7-0.8pp impact
Capital Economics explicitly forecasts sub-2% in April, sub-1% later in 2026
9-month window (April–December) increases probability of at least one sub-2% month

factors_down.factor: BoE (authoritative) forecasts 2.1% in Q2 — just above threshold
Services inflation still 4.3%; sticky; unlikely to collapse quickly
Feb CPI at 3.0% — very far from sub-2%; requires >1pp drop in one month

key_uncertainties: Magnitude of April 2026 Ofgem price cap effect and base effects combining
Whether services inflation remains sticky or falls faster than expected

update_triggers.up: April 2026 ONS CPI release shows reading at or below 2.0%
update_triggers.down: April 2026 CPI release above 2.5%; BoE revises Q2 forecast upward

forecasts.question: Will Spider-Man: Brand New Day gross over $1.5B worldwide by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; trailer hits 1.1B views (all-time record, first film ever); No Way Home $1.9B precedent; July 31 release
forecasts.reference_class: MCU Spider-Man films and billion-dollar franchise sequels with strong pre-release demand signals
forecasts.base_rate: Spider-Man: No Way Home $1.921B; Deadpool & Wolverine $1.338B; MCU films with 700M+ trailer views all exceeded $1B

factors_up.factor: Trailer: 1.1B views — first film trailer ever; 718.6M in first 24hrs (record)
No Way Home precedent: same franchise, same lead, grossed $1.9B
July 31 release gives full summer window plus international rollout

factors_down.factor: MCU box office generally softened since Endgame peak
No prior tracking data; 4 months to release; black-swan risk (quality, competition)
Needs $1.5B — only ~5 MCU films have crossed that threshold

key_uncertainties: Critical reception and word-of-mouth after opening weekend
Competition from other major summer releases

update_triggers.up: Opening weekend tracking shows $200M+ domestic; early reviews 85%+ RT
update_triggers.down: Opening weekend below $150M domestic; critical reception negative

forecasts.question: Will OpenAI and Anthropic have a combined ARR exceeding $70B by 31 December 2026?
forecasts.key_change: ↑ Combined ARR ~$44B (OpenAI $25B + Anthropic $19B); Anthropic 10x/yr growth; crossover projected Aug at ~$43B each
forecasts.reference_class: AI company revenue trajectories at comparable growth stages
forecasts.base_rate: OpenAI $25B ARR (Feb 2026); Anthropic $19B ARR (Mar 2026); combined $44B; need $26B more in 9 months

factors_up.factor: Anthropic growing 10x/year (even at 5x by year-end → $60B+ each)
Epoch AI projects crossover at $43B each in August — combined $86B
Anthropic captures 73% of new enterprise AI spend; enterprise adoption accelerating

factors_down.factor: Both companies note growth is expected to slow from trend rates in 2026
OpenAI expecting $14B cash burn in 2026 — financial pressure may slow investment
Resolution requires credible Q4 2026 reporting — may not be publicly available

key_uncertainties: Whether Anthropic's hypergrowth decelerates materially in H2 2026
Whether Q4 2026 ARR figures are publicly reported before year-end

update_triggers.up: Anthropic Q2 2026 ARR reported above $30B; OpenAI maintains $25B+ trajectory
update_triggers.down: Enterprise AI spend plateaus; either company reports major revenue miss vs internal projections

forecasts.question: Will UK average annual real GDP growth exceed 1.5% across the 2030s (2030-2039)?
forecasts.key_change: → Stable; OBR March 2026 forecasts 1.5% avg over medium-term; AI productivity upside vs persistent structural weakness
forecasts.reference_class: UK long-run GDP growth forecasts and historical performance since financial crisis
forecasts.base_rate: OBR March 2026: 1.5% avg forecast period; long-run assumed 2.1%; post-crisis actual ~1.2-1.5%

factors_up.factor: OBR upside scenario: AI boosts productivity to 1.5% trend — supports >1.5% GDP growth
OBR historical long-run assumption: 2% productivity from 2030+ (consistent with 2.1% GDP)
Global AI deployment could lift all advanced economies above trend

factors_down.factor: OBR March 2026 cuts medium-term TFP to 0.8%; persistent underperformance vs forecast
Structural weaknesses: under-investment, trade frictions, demographic headwinds
Public debt ~95% of GDP by early 2030s constrains fiscal stimulus capacity

key_uncertainties: Whether AI productivity materialises as uplift or remains confined to specific sectors
Whether UK resolves trade relationship frictions with EU and US

update_triggers.up: OBR upgrades medium-term TFP to 1.5%+ in future EFO; UK productivity data beats consensus
update_triggers.down: OBR further downgrades long-run productivity assumption below 0.8%; prolonged fiscal tightening
